Daniel & I will be live on the Futurum Equities YouTube channel at 1PM EST covering:

• Earnings reactions: $ORCL, $ADBE, $RBRK, $PATH
• $NVDA CEO Jensen Huang’s five-layer cake: $TSLA, $PLTR, $NET, $GOOGL, $AMZN, $MSFT, $AVGO, $TSM, $MU, $AMD, $VRT, $OKLO, $CEG, $VST, $ASML
• Nvidia’s $2B bets: $LITE, $COHR, $NBIS, $CRWV
• What the $META Avocado model delay means
• Our thoughts ahead of Nvidia GTC next week

We’ll also leave the last 5–10 minutes for live Q&A, so bring your questions!

$AMZN says AWS and Cerebras will bring faster AI inference to Amazon Bedrock in the coming months.

Bedrock will be the first cloud service to offer Cerebras’ disaggregated inference system using Trainium for prompt processing and CS-3 for token generation.
$MELI has compounded revenue at ~50% CAGR for five years (6x growth) and the stock has gone essentially nowhere.

TOP 5 CRUDE OIL EXPORTERS

• Saudi Arabia ~15%
• Russia ~10%
• United States ~9%
• United Arab Emirates ~9%
• Canada ~9%
$NBIS flying today past $116  👀
$META down 3% after delaying rollout of its new AI model “Avocado” to at least May from a prior March timeline.
$CVNA just announced a 5:1 stock split.

$UBER and Motional are relaunching robotaxi rides in Las Vegas using the Hyundai Ioniq 5.

Riders may be matched with one at no extra cost with safety drivers initially and plans to remove them by year-end.

MACRO DATA JUST DROPPED

• PCE 2.8% YoY vs. Est. 2.9%
• Core PCE 3.1% YoY vs. Est. 2.9%

• Personal spending +0.4% MoM vs. Est. +0.3%
• Personal income +0.4% MoM vs. Est. +0.5%

• U.S. Q4 GDP Revised +0.7 vs. Est. +1.4%

$PLTR says its Maven Smart System is now being deployed across the entire U.S. Department of Defense.

This is Palantir’s flagship defense AI platform for intelligence, targeting & battlefield operations.

$ASML is developing hybrid bonding equipment for next-generation HBM, expanding beyond lithography into advanced packaging.

That puts ASML directly into one of the fastest-growing bottlenecks in the AI semiconductor stack.

$META has delayed its new AI model (code-named Avocado) to at least May after internal tests showed it still trails top models from OpenAI & Anthropic in reasoning, coding & writing.

The report also says Meta has discussed possibly licensing $GOOGL Gemini in the meantime.
Odds of no Fed rate cuts in 2026 have jumped 17 points in two weeks as oil approaches $100  😳

If Iran does this, crude oil prices fall back to bear pre-war levels, and they lose the only leverage they have.
The only reason I do not allow my children to play in the middle of the highway is the cars speeding at 70 MPH. Otherwise, the highway is open as a playground.

War risk insurance was the problem last week, it has largely been resolved.

Technically the Strait is open and they have the insurance to transit it.

The problem is the Strait is being viewed as a "shooting gallery." Iran has hit 16 ships and killed more Mariners than he US Military has lost in this war,

No one wants to be the "duck."

Looking at Goldman Sachs Research gives a good timeline for thinking about the Strait of Hormuz

* March 4, Hormuz flows would recover in 5 days. 

* March 9, they pushed that to 10 days

* March 11, they extended it again to 21 days of very low flows before a 30-day gradual recovery.

Goldman's thinking was similar to mine. On March 4th, I ASSUMED the US Military had an active plan to open the Strait. 

Between March 4th and March 11th, my thinking (like Goldman's) evolved when we both realized they either had the wrong plan or no plan and are currently struggling to figure out what to do.

This is no longer a War with Iran
It is now a War to reopen the Strait of Hormuz
Oil markets are waking up to a new reality: Disruption to the Gulf’s prodigious energy supplies isn’t ending anytime soon on.wsj.com/412QSzw

U.S. Navy Won’t Be Ready To Escort Tankers Through Hormuz For Weeks

Escorting oil tankers in the strait is an incredibly high-risk mission, and might not even be enough to ensure their safe passage.

We are live updating: twz.com/news-features/u-s-na…

He learned little/nothing from the drone warfare between Russia and Ukraine.

Or the Houthis in the Red Sea.

Or Hamas in Gaza against Israel.

They ignored the Ukrainians when they said they have a defensive option against drones.

Generals always prepare for the last war.

This is no longer a war against Iran.

It is now a war to open the Strait of Hormuz

In today's Early Look: "#Quad3 Bond Yield Breakout"

The Signaling Process that I built changed. I changed with it. 

When lightning hits the golf course, do you want to debate last Tuesday's forecast — or get off the fairway?

Incredible advice. Truly. From a man who is never, ever confidently wrong in front of hundreds of millions of people who worship him. Ryan Holiday puts a finer point on it in Wisdom Takes Work: being confidently wrong in private might be forgivable. Doing it in front of a global audience that hangs on your every post? "It's dangerous." (pg 115)

“It’s OK to be wrong, just don’t be confidently wrong.”
-Elon Musk

Meta $META introduced four new generations of custom chips, its MTIA 300, 400, 450 and 500 accelerators for ranking and recommendation training, inference, and genAI workloads. 

Meta says the MTIA 300 for R&R is already in deployment, while the 400 finished testing for genAI models.

$NVDA $AMD $AVGO

Palantir $PLTR stock sold off 38% from November to February, although it fared better than many software peers these past few months. 

I thought it was high-time to revisit the stock given the last earnings report delivered a 60-point acceleration in revenue growth to >70% to assess if the stock has further to go on the downside or if the valuation is (finally) affording investors a better entry. Link in bio.

Nvidia-backed AI cloud startup Nscale raised $2 billion at a $14.6 billion valuation earlier this week, with participation from Nvidia, Dell, Citadel and others. 

The funding is expected to help Nscale expand its AI data center capacity to help meet demand from customers including Microsoft and OpenAI.

$NVDA $DELL $MSFT
